mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals imminent labor market weakness and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during liquidity-driven bull markets or when supply chain disruptions dominate price movements.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price index levels force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 315.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in rolling window analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns (accuracy < 50%) over any 3-year rolling window, or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all tested periods, or signal reverses direction consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and potential supply chain bottlenecks, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in >60% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.05.
